name: Climate Resilience Assessment
intent: Promote comprehensive assessment of observed, projected, and future natural hazards for climate resilience to enhance awareness, increase transparency, reduce vulnerabilities, and ensure long-term safety.
requirements: Complete climate and natural hazard assessment. Identify hazards affecting project site including drought, extreme heat/cold, flooding, hurricanes, hail, landslides, sea level rise, tornadoes, tsunamis, wildfires. Identify 2+ priority hazards with IPCC scenarios, service life, risk ratings, exposure/sensitivity/adaptive capacity analysis.
ai_use_case: Automated hazard identification, risk scoring, IPCC scenario analysis, and resilience report generation
ai_input: Project location coordinates, building type, projected service life, local climate data, historical hazard records
ai_output: Climate resilience assessment report with prioritized hazards, risk matrices, vulnerability analysis, and recommended design strategies
data_sources: NOAA climate data, FEMA flood maps, IPCC AR6 scenarios, local hazard databases, historical weather records

name: Human Impact Assessment
intent: Ensure project development is guided by thorough understanding of social context of local community, workforce, and supply chain, and address potential social inequities.
requirements: Complete assessment covering: Demographics (race, ethnicity, gender, age, income, employment), Local infrastructure/land use (transit, diverse uses, accessibility codes), Human use/health impacts (housing affordability, access to services, community safety), Occupant experience (daylight, views, air/water quality).
ai_use_case: Automated demographic data collection, community analysis, and social impact scoring
ai_input: Project address, census tract, zoning data, local planning documents, accessibility requirements
ai_output: Human impact assessment report with demographic profile, infrastructure analysis, social equity score, and design recommendations
data_sources: US Census Bureau, EPA EJScreen, local planning databases, HUD housing data, transit authority data

name: Carbon Assessment
intent: Understand and reduce long-term direct and indirect carbon emissions including on-site combustion, grid-supplied electricity, refrigerants, and embodied carbon.
requirements: USGBC provides 25-year projection of project emissions from operations, refrigerants, and embodied carbon using data from EAp1, EAp5, MRp2, and optionally LTc4.
ai_use_case: Automated carbon footprint calculation, 25-year emission projections, and decarbonization pathway analysis
ai_input: Energy model outputs, refrigerant schedules, materials quantities, transportation data, grid emission factors
ai_output: 25-year carbon projection report, emission breakdown by source, decarbonization recommendations, comparison to benchmarks
data_sources: Energy models, EC3/Tally for embodied carbon, EPA eGRID, IPCC emission factors

name: Tenant Guidelines
intent: Communicate sustainable design features to tenants and facilitate tenant LEED certification so completed space comprehensively addresses rating system requirements.
requirements: Core and Shell only: Create tenant guidelines describing sustainable features, sustainability goals, guidance for tenant spaces referencing LEED prerequisites, and point of contact for coordination.
ai_use_case: Automated tenant guideline document generation from project specifications
ai_input: Project specifications, LEED credit strategies, base building documentation, contact information
ai_output: Comprehensive tenant guidelines document with sustainable design features, fit-out recommendations, LEED compliance pathways
data_sources: Project specifications, LEED documentation, architectural drawings

name: Integrative Design Process
intent: Support high-performance, cost-effective project outcomes through early analysis of interrelationships among systems. Provide holistic framework for decarbonization, quality of life, and ecological conservation.
requirements: Beginning in predesign through early occupancy: Assemble interdisciplinary team with diverse perspectives, conduct design charrette with owner and 4+ key perspectives, define measurable project goals addressing LEED v5 impact areas, incorporate into OPR.
ai_use_case: AI-facilitated charrette planning, goal tracking dashboard, and synergy identification between building systems
ai_input: Project program, team roster, owner requirements, LEED target certification level
ai_output: Charrette agenda and materials, goal tracking matrix, synergy analysis report, OPR integration checklist
data_sources: Project requirements, energy targets, sustainability goals, team expertise

name: Green Leases
intent: Ensure tenants complete sustainable design features started by base building so completed space comprehensively addresses rating system requirements.
requirements: Core and Shell: Develop standard green lease with tenant fit-out standards, prerequisite compliance for tenant systems (IPp1-3, WEp2, EAp2-4, MRp1, EQp2). Include best practices for 1-6 points or achieve Green Lease Leaders recognition.
ai_use_case: Automated green lease clause generation, compliance tracking, and Green Lease Leaders alignment check
ai_input: Base building specifications, pursued LEED credits, tenant space requirements, Green Lease Leaders criteria
ai_output: Green lease template with required clauses, tenant compliance checklist, points calculation worksheet
data_sources: Green Lease Leaders program requirements, LEED prerequisite requirements, standard lease templates

name: Sensitive Land Protection
intent: Cultivate community resilience by avoiding development of environmentally sensitive lands that provide critical ecosystem services.
requirements: Option 1: Previously developed sites - locate on previously developed land. Option 2: Previously undeveloped sites - avoid prime farmland, floodplains (unless elevated), habitat for threatened species, water bodies (100ft buffer), wetlands (50ft buffer), and protect 40-60% of steep slopes.
ai_use_case: Automated site screening using GIS to identify sensitive lands and verify development eligibility
ai_input: Project site boundaries, parcel data, coordinates
ai_output: Site sensitivity analysis report with maps showing floodplains, wetlands, protected habitats, slope analysis, and compliance determination
data_sources: FEMA flood maps, NWI wetlands data, USFWS critical habitat maps, USDA prime farmland, USGS elevation data

name: Equitable Development
intent: Support economic and social vitality of communities, provide opportunities for community members to live and work in proximity, encourage development in areas with developmental challenges.
requirements: Option 1: Priority Sites (brownfield remediation 2pts, historic location 1pt). Option 2: Housing and Jobs Proximity (support local economy 1pt, location-efficient affordable housing 2pts). Option 3: Equitable Construction (life-skills training, apprenticeship 2pts).
ai_use_case: Automated site classification for brownfields/historic districts, local workforce analysis, and affordable housing proximity mapping
ai_input: Site address, parcel history, local workforce data, housing market data
ai_output: Equitable development pathway analysis with achievable options, brownfield status verification, historic district confirmation, local hiring recommendations
data_sources: EPA brownfields database, National Register of Historic Places, local historic registries, Census employment data, HUD housing data

name: Compact and Connected Development
intent: Conserve land and ecosystem resources by encouraging development in areas with existing infrastructure. Promote livability, walkability, and transportation efficiency.
requirements: Option 1: Surrounding Density (7+ DU/acre or 22,000+ SF/acre) 1-2pts. Option 2: Access to Transit (weekday/weekend trips) 1-4pts. Option 3: Walkable Location (pedestrian-oriented, Walk Score 70+) 1-3pts.
ai_use_case: Automated density calculations, transit accessibility scoring, and walkability analysis
ai_input: Site address, surrounding parcel data, transit routes and schedules
ai_output: Density analysis with dwelling units/acre, transit accessibility score with trip counts, Walk Score, recommended credit pathway and points
data_sources: Local assessor data, GTFS transit feeds, Walk Score API, census data

name: Transportation Demand Management
intent: Reduce pollution and land development effects from automobile use through alternative transportation. Promote livable and healthy communities through reduced VMT.
requirements: Transportation Demand Assessment required (estimate VMT, baseline/proposed carbon emissions). Option 1: Parking (reduce 30-100%, parking fee) 1-3pts. Option 2: Active Travel Facilities (bicycle storage, showers, bike maintenance) 1-3pts.
ai_use_case: Automated VMT calculation, carbon emission modeling, parking ratio analysis, and bicycle facility sizing
ai_input: Project type, occupancy, parking ratios, local VMT data, transit accessibility
ai_output: Transportation demand assessment with VMT estimates, carbon emission baseline/proposed, parking reduction analysis, bicycle facility requirements
data_sources: ITE Parking Generation Manual, regional VMT data, EPA emission factors

name: Electric Vehicles
intent: Encourage use of EVs and infrastructure. Reduce negative health effects by lowering GHG emissions from conventionally fueled vehicles.
requirements: Option 1: Install EVSE (Level 2/3, ENERGY STAR connected functionality, accessible space) 5-15% of parking for 1-2pts. Option 2: EV Readiness (40-amp panel, conduit, junction box) 10-20% for 1pt.
ai_use_case: Automated EVSE quantity calculation, electrical load analysis, and accessible parking compliance check
ai_input: Total parking spaces, building type (commercial/residential), electrical capacity
ai_output: EVSE requirement calculation, electrical load estimate, accessible EV space design requirements, points achievable
data_sources: Parking counts, electrical capacity data, ENERGY STAR EVSE registry

name: Minimized Site Disturbance
intent: Limit site disturbance from construction activities and preserve existing native vegetation, healthy soils, and wildlife habitats.
requirements: Erosion & Sedimentation Control Plan per EPA 2022 CGP or equivalent. Site Assessment: conserve 100% special-status vegetation, identify healthy plant communities with exclusion zones, address invasive species removal.
ai_use_case: Automated erosion control plan generation, vegetation mapping from satellite imagery, invasive species identification
ai_input: Site survey, topography, drainage patterns, vegetation inventory
ai_output: Erosion and sedimentation control plan, vegetation assessment report, construction exclusion zone maps, invasive species management plan
data_sources: Site surveys, USDA plant databases, invasive species registries, EPA CGP requirements

name: Biodiverse Habitat
intent: Conserve existing natural areas, enhance biodiversity, restore damaged areas, and provide thriving habitats for local wildlife.
requirements: Option 1: Preserve 40% greenfield area OR restore 20-40% previously disturbed sites with soil and vegetation restoration. Option 2: Bird-friendly glass (treat 90%+ of facade risk zones).
ai_use_case: Automated habitat area calculations, soil restoration specifications, bird collision risk facade analysis
ai_input: Site plan, existing vegetation survey, facade drawings, glass specifications
ai_output: Habitat preservation/restoration area calculations, soil restoration specifications, bird collision threat factor analysis, glass treatment requirements
data_sources: Site surveys, native plant databases, bird collision research, glass manufacturer data

name: Accessible Outdoor Space
intent: Provide equitably designed outdoor spaces that are welcoming to all, encourage interaction with nature, and foster human health and well-being.
requirements: Provide accessible outdoor space ≥30% of total site area (25% zero lot line). Space must have physically accessible path, shade structures, seating (1 seat/250 SF outdoor), and include community/cultural/acoustic elements.
ai_use_case: Automated outdoor space area calculations, seating requirement calculations, accessibility path compliance checking
ai_input: Site plan, building footprint, outdoor space design, accessibility routes
ai_output: Outdoor space percentage calculation, seating requirements, accessibility compliance checklist, community amenity recommendations
data_sources: Site plans, accessibility codes, outdoor furniture standards

name: Rainwater Management
intent: Reduce runoff volume and improve water quality by replicating natural hydrology based on historical conditions and undeveloped ecosystems.
requirements: Option 1: Retain 80-90th percentile rainfall on site using LID/GI practices for 1-3 points (+1 for water reuse). Option 2: Retain increase in runoff volume from natural land cover conditions for 3 points.
ai_use_case: Automated stormwater calculations, LID/GI sizing, rainfall analysis, and runoff modeling
ai_input: Site area, impervious coverage, local rainfall data, soil infiltration rates
ai_output: Rainfall percentile calculations, required retention volume, LID/GI sizing recommendations, runoff comparison to natural conditions
data_sources: NOAA rainfall data, local stormwater manuals, soil surveys

name: Enhanced Resilient Site Design
intent: Reduce risk of catastrophic impacts from natural and climate events by designing sites resilient to observed, projected, and future hazards.
requirements: Address 2+ high-priority hazards from IPp1 with specific design strategies: drought (water efficiency, drought-tolerant plants), extreme heat (shade, evaporative cooling, high-SR paving), flooding, hurricanes, landslides, wildfires, winter storms.
ai_use_case: Automated resilience strategy matching based on identified hazards from IPp1, specification generation
ai_input: IPp1 hazard assessment results, site design parameters, local codes
ai_output: Resilience strategy matrix matched to priority hazards, design specifications for each strategy, compliance verification checklist
data_sources: IPp1 assessment, SITES rating system, local resilience codes

name: Heat Island Reduction
intent: Mitigate disparate impacts on microclimates and habitats caused by heat islands and extreme heat events.
requirements: Option 1: Weighted nonroof/roof approach with shade, high-reflectance roofing, vegetated roofs. Option 2: Parking under cover (75%+ under roof, under/in structure, or covered with open-grid). Option 3: Tree equity analysis with canopy increase.
ai_use_case: Automated SRI calculations, shade coverage analysis, Tree Equity Score lookup, weighted compliance calculation
ai_input: Site plan, roof areas, paving areas, material specifications, Tree Equity Score (US only)
ai_output: Heat island compliance calculations, weighted area analysis, material SRI requirements, Tree Equity recommendations
data_sources: Material SRI databases, American Forests Tree Equity Score, site measurements

name: Light Pollution Reduction
intent: Increase night sky access, improve nighttime visibility, and reduce consequences of development for wildlife and people.
requirements: Meet uplight ratings (U0-U4 based on lighting zone), light trespass backlight/glare ratings per IES TM-15-11, internally illuminated signage limits (200-600 nits average, 500-1200 nits max based on zone).
ai_use_case: Automated luminaire BUG rating verification, lighting zone determination, signage luminance compliance
ai_input: Luminaire schedules with BUG ratings, lighting zone, signage specifications
ai_output: Lighting compliance report with uplight, backlight, glare ratings for each luminaire, signage luminance compliance, non-compliant fixture identification
data_sources: Luminaire cut sheets, IES TM-15-11 standards, lighting zone maps

name: Water Metering and Reporting
intent: Conserve potable water resources, support water management, and identify opportunities for additional water savings by tracking consumption.
requirements: Install permanent water meters for total potable and alternative water sources. Facility manager/tenants must access data. Commit to sharing whole-project water data with USGBC for 5 years.
ai_use_case: Automated meter specification, data commitment documentation, reporting system setup
ai_input: Building water systems, alternative water sources, facility management structure
ai_output: Water metering plan, meter specifications, data sharing commitment documentation, reporting protocol
data_sources: Plumbing design, water system inventory, USGBC reporting requirements

name: Minimum Water Efficiency
intent: Reduce potable water consumption and associated energy/carbon emissions through efficiency-first approach.
requirements: Fixture/fittings: Prescriptive maximum flow rates OR 20% calculated reduction. Equipment: ENERGY STAR appliances, once-through cooling prohibition, process water efficiency. Outdoor: No irrigation OR 30% reduction from TIR baseline.
ai_use_case: Automated fixture flow rate compliance checking, water use calculations, irrigation requirement analysis
ai_input: Fixture schedules, appliance specifications, landscape areas, plant types
ai_output: Fixture compliance report, water use baseline and proposed calculations, irrigation water requirement analysis, equipment efficiency verification
data_sources: Fixture manufacturer data, ENERGY STAR databases, EPA TIR methodology

name: Water Metering and Leak Detection
intent: Conserve potable water, support water management, limit material waste from leaks, and identify additional savings opportunities.
requirements: Option 1: Submeters for indoor plumbing (80%+), irrigation, makeup water, commercial kitchen (100+ meals/day), laundry (120,000+ lb/year). Option 2: Leak detection sensors for irrigation, 50% flush fixtures, and makeup water systems.
ai_use_case: Automated submeter placement planning, leak detection system specification, action plan generation
ai_input: Building water systems, fixture counts, irrigation system, process water systems
ai_output: Submetering plan with meter locations, leak detection sensor locations, data integration requirements, leak response action plan
data_sources: Plumbing design, fixture schedules, meal counts, laundry projections

name: Enhanced Water Efficiency
intent: Further reduce potable water consumption and reward use of alternative water sources to preserve resources.
requirements: Option 1: Whole-project water use reduction 30-65% for 1-8 points. Options 2-6: Fixture reduction, appliance/process efficiency, outdoor water reduction, cooling optimization, water reuse systems.
ai_use_case: Automated whole-project water modeling, alternative water source analysis, optimization pathway recommendations
ai_input: All water uses (indoor, outdoor, process), alternative water availability (rainwater, graywater, reclaimed)
ai_output: Whole-project water model, reduction percentage calculations, alternative water integration analysis, optimal pathway recommendation
data_sources: Water use projections, alternative water availability, local water reuse regulations

name: Operational Carbon Projection and Decarbonization Plan
intent: Enable stakeholders to visualize how design decisions impact long-term operational carbon emissions and ensure planning for low-carbon outcomes.
requirements: Design analysis with energy simulation, site energy estimate (EUI), carbon projection review, decarbonization plan via Path 1 (design for electrification) OR Path 2 (plan for decarbonization with timeline).
ai_use_case: Automated energy simulation integration, carbon projection generation, decarbonization pathway analysis
ai_input: Energy model outputs, grid emission factors, fuel types, electrification options
ai_output: Operational carbon projection, 25-year emission trajectory, decarbonization pathway comparison, electrification vs. fuel timeline
data_sources: Energy models, EPA eGRID, IPCC emission factors, grid decarbonization projections

name: Minimum Energy Efficiency
intent: Establish minimum energy efficiency of building envelope and systems and reduce associated environmental and economic harm.
requirements: Meet ASHRAE 90.1-2019/2022 prescriptive OR performance requirements. Energy simulation must show compliance with baseline building. Residential projects follow alternative paths.
ai_use_case: Automated energy code compliance checking, prescriptive path verification, performance path baseline comparison
ai_input: Building envelope specifications, HVAC systems, lighting design, energy model
ai_output: Code compliance report, prescriptive requirement checklist, performance comparison to baseline, non-compliance identification
data_sources: ASHRAE 90.1, building specifications, energy model

name: Fundamental Commissioning
intent: Ensure building systems function as designed at the time of completion through fundamental commissioning.
requirements: Comply with ASHRAE 90.1 commissioning requirements. Designate CxP by end of DD phase. CxP develops OPR, reviews BOD, attends milestone meetings, develops ongoing Cx plan.
ai_use_case: Automated Cx milestone tracking, OPR template generation, documentation management
ai_input: Project schedule, design milestones, system types, owner requirements
ai_output: Commissioning plan template, milestone schedule, OPR framework, documentation checklist, meeting agendas
data_sources: ASHRAE 90.1, ASHRAE 202, project specifications

name: Energy Metering and Reporting
intent: Support energy management and facilitate identification of ongoing opportunities for energy and GHG savings through tracking and reporting.
requirements: Install energy monitoring per ASHRAE 90.1. Report energy data to USGBC for 5 years via Arc platform. Commit to ongoing measurement and reporting.
ai_use_case: Automated metering specification, Arc platform integration setup, reporting system configuration
ai_input: Energy systems, metering points, building type, Arc platform requirements
ai_output: Metering plan, Arc platform setup documentation, reporting schedule, data commitment forms
data_sources: ASHRAE 90.1 metering requirements, Arc platform specifications

name: Fundamental Refrigerant Management
intent: Reduce ozone depletion and support early compliance with refrigerant phase-out requirements.
requirements: No CFC-based refrigerants in new HVAC&R systems. Phase-out plan for existing CFC refrigerants by 2025. Comply with refrigerant GWP limits and Montreal Protocol schedules.
ai_use_case: Automated refrigerant GWP checking, phase-out schedule verification, compliance documentation
ai_input: HVAC equipment schedules, refrigerant types, existing system inventory
ai_output: Refrigerant compliance report, GWP calculations, phase-out schedule (if applicable), compliant equipment recommendations
data_sources: Montreal Protocol schedules, EPA refrigerant regulations, equipment manufacturer data

name: Electrification
intent: Reduce direct carbon emissions from buildings and enable access to renewable energy by electrifying building systems.
requirements: Electrify heating systems with weighted average COP ≥1.8. Options: Full electrification (5pts NC, 4pts CS), low-temperature exception (3-4pts), electrification readiness (1pt).
ai_use_case: Automated COP calculation, electrification pathway analysis, equipment specification recommendations
ai_input: HVAC equipment schedules, heating loads, climate zone, hot water systems
ai_output: Weighted average COP calculation, electrification pathway recommendation, equipment specifications, points achievable
data_sources: Equipment performance data, manufacturer specifications, climate data

name: Reduce Peak Thermal Loads
intent: Minimize demand on grid resources and improve building resilience through reduced peak loads.
requirements: Option 1: Infiltration and balanced ventilation (1pt). Option 2: Ventilation energy recovery (1pt). Option 3: Thermal bridging (1pt). Option 4: Peak thermal load reductions via modeling (1-3pts).
ai_use_case: Automated envelope analysis, energy recovery sizing, thermal bridge detection, peak load modeling
ai_input: Building envelope specifications, ventilation design, energy model, thermal bridge details
ai_output: Air leakage compliance analysis, ERV sizing verification, thermal bridge assessment, peak load reduction calculations
data_sources: Energy models, envelope specifications, ERV manufacturer data

name: Enhanced Energy Efficiency
intent: Reduce environmental and economic harm from excessive energy use by achieving increasing levels of energy efficiency.
requirements: Performance Path: Demonstrate 6-50%+ improvement over baseline for 1-10 points. Prescriptive Path: Meet enhanced prescriptive requirements for envelope, lighting, HVAC for up to 10 points.
ai_use_case: Automated energy model comparison to baseline, prescriptive compliance checking, optimization recommendations
ai_input: Energy model, baseline model, building specifications, climate zone
ai_output: Percent improvement calculation, points achievable, optimization opportunities, prescriptive compliance report
data_sources: Energy models, ASHRAE 90.1, Appendix G

name: Renewable Energy
intent: Reduce environmental and economic harm from fossil fuel energy by increasing self-supply of renewable energy.
requirements: Tier 1: On-site renewables (5-100%+ of energy). Tier 2: Off-site new renewables (up to 100%). Tier 3: Green-e certified RECs. CS Option 2: Solar-ready roof with infrastructure.
ai_use_case: Automated renewable energy production estimation, REC verification, solar-ready analysis
ai_input: Building energy use, roof area, solar resource data, REC procurement plans
ai_output: On-site renewable potential analysis, percent of energy offset, REC requirements, solar-ready infrastructure specifications
data_sources: Solar resource data (NREL PVWatts), energy model, Green-e registry

name: Enhanced Commissioning
intent: Further ensure building systems function as designed and maintain energy performance over time.
requirements: Option 1: Enhanced Cx for MEP (ASHRAE 202) 2pts AND/OR building enclosure (ASTM E2947) 1pt. Option 2: Monitoring-based Cx with analytics software 1-2pts.
ai_use_case: Automated MBCx data analytics, fault detection, performance tracking dashboards
ai_input: BMS data streams, energy consumption, system performance parameters
ai_output: MBCx analytics dashboard, fault detection alerts, performance trend reports, optimization recommendations
data_sources: BMS data, energy meters, system sensors

name: Grid Interactive
intent: Enable building to participate in grid services, reduce peak demand, and support grid reliability.
requirements: Option 1: Demand response capable (1pt). Option 2: Energy storage (1pt). Option 3: Smart grid integration with automated load management.
ai_use_case: Automated demand response capability verification, storage sizing analysis, grid integration documentation
ai_input: Building loads, DR-capable equipment, storage specifications, utility DR programs
ai_output: DR capability assessment, storage sizing analysis, grid integration specifications, utility program alignment
data_sources: Equipment specifications, utility DR programs, storage manufacturer data

name: Enhanced Refrigerant Management
intent: Further reduce ozone depletion and global warming potential from refrigerants.
requirements: Option 1: Low-GWP refrigerants below calculated threshold (1-2pts based on reduction). Option 2: No refrigerants (2pts). Option 3: Refrigerant leak detection for large systems.
ai_use_case: Automated GWP impact calculation, low-GWP equipment recommendations, leak detection system specification
ai_input: HVAC equipment schedules, refrigerant charges, system types
ai_output: Total GWP impact calculation, comparison to threshold, low-GWP equipment alternatives, leak detection requirements
data_sources: Refrigerant GWP values, equipment databases, manufacturer specifications

name: Planning for Zero Waste Operations
intent: Reduce waste generated by occupants through reduction, reuse, and recycling services. Set building up for zero waste operations.
requirements: Provide dedicated recyclables collection/storage for organics, paper, cardboard, glass, plastics, metals. Safe disposal for batteries, mercury lamps, e-waste. Zero waste operations planning with design details for occupants.
ai_use_case: Automated waste stream analysis, storage area sizing, recycling service availability check
ai_input: Building type, occupancy, local recycling services, floor plans
ai_output: Waste stream projections, recycling storage sizing, service availability report, zero waste operations plan template
data_sources: Waste generation rates, local hauler services, building codes

name: Quantify and Assess Embodied Carbon
intent: Quantify embodied carbon impacts of structure, enclosure, and hardscape and assess top sources.
requirements: Quantify GWP for structure, enclosure, hardscape including asphalt, concrete, masonry, steel, insulation, aluminum, wood, cladding, glass. Calculate cradle-to-gate (A1-A3) emissions. Identify top 3 carbon sources with reduction strategies.
ai_use_case: Automated bill of materials extraction, embodied carbon calculation, hot spot identification
ai_input: Structural drawings, material specifications, quantities take-off
ai_output: Bill of materials with GWP values, total embodied carbon, top 3 hot spots identification, reduction strategy recommendations
data_sources: EC3 database, CLF Material Baselines, EPD databases, industry averages

name: Building and Materials Reuse
intent: Incorporate reused materials to reduce embodied carbon, keep materials in circularity, reduce demand for virgin materials.
requirements: Option 1: Building Reuse - maintain 20-50%+ existing structure/enclosure for 1-3 points. Option 2: Materials Reuse - use salvaged/reused nonstructural materials (minimum $5,000 value) for 1-2 points.
ai_use_case: Automated existing building area calculations, salvage material tracking, reuse percentage documentation
ai_input: Existing building survey, demolition plans, salvage material inventory
ai_output: Building reuse percentage calculation, salvage material valuation, compliance documentation
data_sources: Existing building surveys, salvage inventories, market values

name: Reduce Embodied Carbon
intent: Track and reduce embodied carbon of major structural, enclosure, and hardscape materials.
requirements: Option 1: WBLCA showing 5-40%+ reduction from baseline for 1-6 points. Option 2: EPD analysis with project-average or materials-type approach for 1-3 points. Option 3: Track construction emissions (A5) for 1-2 points.
ai_use_case: Automated WBLCA analysis, EPD collection and analysis, construction emission tracking
ai_input: Material quantities, EPDs, baseline project data, construction fuel/energy use
ai_output: WBLCA results with reduction percentage, EPD analysis summary, construction emission report, points achievable
data_sources: WBLCA tools (Tally, One Click LCA), EC3 database, EPD registries

name: Low-Emitting Materials
intent: Reduce concentrations of chemical contaminants that damage air quality. Protect health of installers and occupants.
requirements: Specify products meeting low-emitting criteria for paints/coatings, flooring, ceilings, adhesives/sealants, walls, insulation, composite wood, furniture. Path 1: 3 categories at 90%+ for 1pt. Path 2: +2 additional categories at 80%+ for 2pts.
ai_use_case: Automated product specification verification against emission standards, compliance tracking
ai_input: Product specifications, manufacturer certifications, product submittals
ai_output: Product compliance matrix by category, compliant/non-compliant product lists, percentage achievement per category
data_sources: CDPH Standard Method, GREENGUARD, FloorScore, HPD databases

name: Building Product Selection and Procurement
intent: Encourage use of products for which information on life-cycle impacts is available. Reward products verified to have improved impacts.
requirements: Products from 5+ manufacturers with EPDs, health declarations (HPDs), and/or responsible sourcing certifications. Points based on number of products meeting criteria across categories.
ai_use_case: Automated product documentation verification, manufacturer EPD/HPD tracking, certification validation
ai_input: Product specifications, manufacturer documentation, certification claims
ai_output: Product documentation matrix, EPD/HPD inventory, certification summary, points achievable by pathway
data_sources: EPD registries, HPD Collaborative, FSC, Cradle to Cradle

name: Construction and Demolition Waste Diversion
intent: Reduce waste disposed in landfills by recovering materials for recycling and reuse.
requirements: Divert 50%+ C&D waste from landfill by weight for 1 point. Divert 75%+ for 2 points. Track and report waste by material stream.
ai_use_case: Automated waste tracking, diversion rate calculation, material stream reporting
ai_input: Waste manifests, hauler reports, material weights
ai_output: Waste tracking dashboard, diversion rate calculations, material stream summary, compliance documentation
data_sources: Waste manifests, hauler reports, facility recycling rates

name: Construction Management
intent: Minimize exposure of construction workers to harmful pollutants and extreme heat during construction.
requirements: Implement construction IAQ plan with source control, pathway interruption, housekeeping, and scheduling. Address heat illness prevention for workers.
ai_use_case: Automated IAQ plan generation, heat index monitoring, compliance documentation
ai_input: Construction schedule, site conditions, weather data, material delivery schedule
ai_output: Construction IAQ management plan, heat illness prevention plan, monitoring schedule, documentation templates
data_sources: Weather data, OSHA heat guidelines, IAQ best practices

name: Fundamental Air Quality
intent: Design for above-average indoor air quality to support occupant health and well-being.
requirements: Investigate regional/local air quality per ASHRAE 62.1. Meet ASHRAE 62.1 ventilation and filtration (MERV 13+). Design entryway systems with walk-off mats. Healthcare meets ASHRAE 170.
ai_use_case: Automated ventilation rate calculations, filtration verification, outdoor air quality investigation
ai_input: Space types, occupancy, outdoor air quality data, HVAC design
ai_output: Ventilation rate calculations, filtration compliance verification, outdoor air quality report, entryway design requirements
data_sources: ASHRAE 62.1, EPA AQI data, equipment specifications

name: No Smoking or Vehicle Idling
intent: Prevent exposure of building occupants to environmental tobacco smoke and vehicle exhaust.
requirements: Prohibit smoking and e-cigarette use in building and within 25 feet of entries, outdoor air intakes, and operable windows. Prohibit vehicle idling within 25 feet of entries and air intakes.
ai_use_case: Automated signage location planning, policy document generation, compliance zone mapping
ai_input: Building entries, air intake locations, operable windows, site plan
ai_output: No-smoking zone map, signage location plan, policy document template, compliance verification checklist
data_sources: Architectural drawings, site plans

name: Enhanced Air Quality
intent: Design for increased IAQ to better protect health of building occupants.
requirements: Option 1: Increase ventilation by 15%+ above ASHRAE 62.1 minimum. Option 2: Enhanced IAQ design meeting lower limits for PM2.5, formaldehyde, and ozone.
ai_use_case: Automated ventilation increase calculations, IAQ design verification against enhanced limits
ai_input: Baseline ventilation rates, HVAC design, IAQ design targets
ai_output: Enhanced ventilation calculations, IAQ design compliance verification, points achievable
data_sources: ASHRAE 62.1, enhanced IAQ limits, HVAC design

name: Occupant Experience
intent: Move beyond neutral spaces toward human-centered design supporting customization, enjoyment, and emotional connections.
requirements: Option 1: Biophilic design (1pt) and quality views (2-3pts). Option 2: Adaptable environment (1pt). Option 3: Thermal environment per ASHRAE 55 (1pt). Option 4: Acoustic mapping/criteria (1-2pts). Option 5: Lighting - glare, electric lighting, daylight (1-6pts). Max 7 points.
ai_use_case: Automated daylight simulation, view quality analysis, acoustic mapping, thermal comfort analysis
ai_input: Building geometry, glazing specifications, interior layouts, site surroundings
ai_output: Daylight analysis results, view quality assessment, acoustic zone mapping, thermal comfort compliance, points achievable by option
data_sources: Building geometry, site context, ASHRAE 55, acoustic standards

name: Accessibility and Inclusion
intent: Create inclusive environments accessible to all, regardless of age, size, or ability.
requirements: Go beyond ADA minimum with strategies for: cognitive/neurological accessibility, mobility accessibility, safety/aging accommodations, social health features, and navigation/wayfinding.
ai_use_case: Automated accessibility checklist generation, inclusive design feature tracking
ai_input: Floor plans, design specifications, accessibility goals
ai_output: Enhanced accessibility checklist, inclusive design feature matrix, compliance gap analysis
data_sources: ADA standards, inclusive design best practices

name: Resilient Spaces
intent: Support design features that increase capacity for occupants to adapt to changing climate conditions and protect from events compromising indoor environment.
requirements: Option 1: Management mode for episodic outdoor conditions (wildfire smoke). Option 2: Management mode for respiratory diseases. Option 3: Thermal safety during power outages (extreme heat/cold). Option 4: Operable windows (50-75% of spaces).
ai_use_case: Automated resilient space design verification, operable window analysis, thermal safety modeling
ai_input: HVAC design, building envelope, operable window specifications, priority hazards from IPp1
ai_output: Resilience strategy verification, operable window coverage calculation, thermal safety zone identification
data_sources: IPp1 hazard assessment, HVAC design, window schedules

name: Air Quality Testing and Monitoring
intent: Support better management of IAQ and identify opportunities for health-based approaches to building operations.
requirements: Option 1: Preoccupancy air testing for PM2.5, PM10, CO, ozone (1pt), and/or VOCs (1pt). Option 2: Continuous IAQ monitoring with sensors for PM2.5, CO2, and TVOC.
ai_use_case: Automated testing protocol generation, monitoring system specification, results analysis
ai_input: Building area, space types, testing requirements, monitoring system options
ai_output: Testing protocol with measurement locations, monitoring system specifications, compliance thresholds, results analysis framework
data_sources: LEED testing thresholds, sensor specifications, testing standards

name: Project Priorities
intent: Address project-specific goals and regional priorities while rewarding exemplary performance and innovation.
requirements: Earn points through: Regional Priority credits, Project-Type credits, Exemplary Performance credits, Pilot Credits, and Innovative Strategies (up to 9 points total).
ai_use_case: Automated regional priority credit identification, exemplary performance pathway analysis
ai_input: Project location, pursued credits, project type, innovation ideas
ai_output: Regional priority credit recommendations, exemplary performance opportunities, pilot credit eligibility, innovation strategy framework
data_sources: USGBC Regional Priority credit database, Project Priority Library, pilot credit library

name: LEED AP
intent: Encourage team integration required by a LEED AP and streamline application and certification process.
requirements: At least one principal participant must be a LEED AP with specialty appropriate for the project.
ai_use_case: Automated credential verification, team roster tracking
ai_input: Team roster, credential information
ai_output: LEED AP verification, credential status report
data_sources: GBCI credential directory
